title: Red Zinger Mini Classic 1989 boys 13 Jamestown

transcript.text: We are at the Jamestown. This is the finish line that they'll be coming across. It's quite a long eight-mile climb, just a real gentle upward, lots of hip-hop-in-turns as they're going to be coming up.
It's really kind of a cute little town. There's quite a few people, and this is the little town. Fire station, a little mercantile, a bank, and a post office, and a gift shop, and that's about it.
One of those cute little towns up in the hills. There's some of the race officials saying they're enjoying resting a little bit before they start out again.
Over there are these blocks. One, two, three. No, thanks. We'll pass on the banana, thanks.
There's Josh Peterson here in the area. We are. Her big brother is racing with Mike.
